data.title: Gate DEX BountyDrop launches airdrop events for Anichess and Neura.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Gate DEX's BountyDrop has launched the Anichess and Neura airdrop events. Users can participate in the tasks through the BountyDrop module on the Gate DEX "Events" page. 2,000 participants who complete the Anichess task will be randomly selected to share $10,000 CHECK; 1,000 participants who complete the Neura task will be randomly selected to share 10,000 USDT. In addition, Gate DEX is running the "SOL Chain Newcomer Carnival" event from January 19th to February 5th. During the event, new Gate Wallet SOL Chain Swap users who complete their first transaction will receive a gas fee subsidy. Furthermore, users who invite friends to participate will receive a 30% transaction rebate, and invited friends will receive a 20% transaction rebate. Gate DEX provides a one-stop connection for 100+ public chains, millions of assets, and tens of thousands of DApps, offering optimal solutions for every Web3 need. BountyDrop is a dedicated earning activity section on the Gate DEX discovery page, where users can complete simple tasks for a chance to win rewards or participate in a lottery for airdropped tokens.

data.title: Analysts: Amid heightened macroeconomic uncertainty, institutional investors are tightening their risk exposure.
data.description: The US spot Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of $708.7 million yesterday, the largest single-day outflow in nearly two months, while the Ethereum ETF saw a net outflow of $286.9 million. Amidst heightened macroeconomic uncertainty, institutional investors are further reducing their risk exposure. Rachael Lucas, a crypto analyst at BTC Markets, stated that Wednesday's outflows were more of a typical "risk-off" behavior. She pointed out that when the macroeconomic environment turns unfavorable, such as rising interest rates, escalating geopolitical risks, or a sudden increase in market volatility, institutional investors tend to be the first to withdraw funds from high-beta assets. This is not a signal of structural weakness, but rather institutions tightening their risk exposure before uncertainty sets in, and does not mean abandoning the crypto asset class altogether. (The Block)

data.title: ING: Inflation may reshape the Bank of Japan's interest rate hike plans
data.description: Japan's CPI data, due ahead of Friday's interest rate decision, is expected to show a significant drop in December's inflation rate. Analysts at ING Bank stated that a decline in inflation could prompt the Bank of Japan to reassess its future interest rate hike plans. "Strong wage growth and government aid measures will keep core inflation above 2%. Once the Bank of Japan confirms that core inflation will consistently remain above 2% and higher than overall inflation, it is likely to take further action sometime in the second half of 2026." (Jinshi)

data.title: European Pension Funds Divest from U.S. Treasury Bonds Amid Fiscal Concerns
data.description: Swedish pension fund Alecta has divested between $7.7 billion and $8.8 billion in U.S. Treasury bonds since early 2025. According to NS3.AI, this decision was driven by fiscal unpredictability and the increasing U.S. federal debt. Similarly, Denmark’s AkademikerPension has announced its intention to withdraw its entire $100 million investment in U.S. Treasury bonds. These actions reflect a broader concern among European institutional investors regarding the sustainability of American fiscal policy. The divestments occur in the context of escalating geopolitical tensions, prompting further debate over the future of U.S. fiscal stability.

data.title: The Smarter Web Company Increases Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: The Smarter Web Company, a publicly listed firm in the United Kingdom, has increased its Bitcoin holdings by acquiring an additional 10 Bitcoins. According to Foresight News, this acquisition brings the company's total Bitcoin holdings to 2,674.

data.title: Cardano's Trading Volume Drops Sharply Amid Whale Accumulation
data.description: Cardano's trading volume on decentralized exchanges has experienced a significant decline, dropping over 95% in just two weeks. According to NS3.AI, this decrease indicates a notable reduction in retail participation. Despite the downturn, large holders of ADA, often referred to as whales, have been accumulating millions of tokens during the price drop, indicating their confidence in a potential market rebound. Concurrently, the derivatives markets are showing a strong short bias, which could lead to short squeezes if ADA's price surpasses key resistance levels.

At the center of the effort is a proposal to ban interest-bearing stablecoins, a move the banking lobby argues is necessary to protect the […] The post U. S

data.title: Pundi AI partners with Presens Network to build real-world contexts for AI.
data.description: Pundi AI announced a collaboration with Presens Network, the world's first decentralized spatiotemporal data network. This collaboration combines Pundi AI's decentralized data infrastructure with Presens' global presence network, introducing verifiable real-world contexts into AI. Presens, through its global network spanning 141 countries, over 20,000 Pulse nodes, and 80,000 presence locations, transforms human activity into machine-readable spatiotemporal data, creating a global-scale spatiotemporal intelligence layer. This collaboration brings Presens' real-time presence data to Pundi AI's data platform and marketplace, providing AI developers with new data types that reflect real-world activities, making AI more relevant to reality and humanity. Data contributors will be rewarded, while developers will gain access to trusted, verified datasets. Both parties are committed to maintaining data openness, decentralization, and user ownership, jointly driving the connection between the physical world and decentralized AI infrastructure, and realizing a more open and human-centric AI future.
